title: Calathea oblonga
taxonomy: Calathea oblonga is classified as a member of the Marantaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the prayer plant family. This family includes over 600 species of plants, many of which are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Calathea oblonga is also classified as a member of the genus Calathea, which is a group of ornamental plants known for their attractive foliage and vibrant colors. The genus Calathea is further divided into several species, including the Calathea oblonga, which is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Overall, the classification of Calathea oblonga reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the broader family of flowering plants.
